sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports headaches, ringing in their ears, and blurred vision. He also reports experiencing numbness and tingling intermittently in his extremities. The patient enjoys running.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals papilledema on fundoscopic examination. Blood pressure is 118/72 mmHg. Heart rate is 78 bpm.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's symptoms are concerning for idiopathic intracranial hypertension and possible peripheral neuropathy. He has papilledema, which supports the diagnosis of IIH.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Start oral acetazolamide 250 mg twice daily. Order MRI of brain with contrast to evaluate for IIH. Schedule neurology follow-up in 2 weeks.
